source_item.title: US Senate bill seeks 100% tariffs on India, 4 other nations for buying Russian oil

slide1_hook.label: US Senate wants 100% tariffs on India
slide1_hook.subhead: The target: countries still buying Russian oil

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: India kept buying discounted Russian crude
slide2_backstory.body: Since the Ukraine war began, Russia has sold oil to Asia at steep discounts. India and China became the biggest buyers, and India says it needs the cheap oil for energy security.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Bipartisan bill targets India, China, three others
slide3_news.body: A bipartisan bill from Senators Richard Blumenthal and the late Lindsey Graham has over 60 backers in Congress. It proposes 100% tariffs on the top 5 buyers of Russian oil and gas: India, China, Slovakia, Hungary, and Azerbaijan.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Tariffs as a geopolitical weapon, first time
bullets: Congress's first use of tariffs to punish a war
Aims to cut off Russia's war funding
Could strain India's energy trade with the US

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Should India keep buying discounted Russian oil even if it risks 100% US tariffs?
slide5_engage.cta: Tell us where you stand and follow for more policy updates
